diffs.patch:
Index: bench-artifacts/routing-read-write-ledger.md
===================================================================
--- bench-artifacts/routing-read-write-ledger.md	
+++ bench-artifacts/routing-read-write-ledger.md	
@@ -0,0 +1,130 @@
+# Routing Read/Write Ledger
+
+## src/hono.ts
+- Purpose: Main Hono class entry point that extends HonoBase and configures the default SmartRouter with RegExpRouter and TrieRouter fallbacks.
+- Names noticed: Hono, HonoBase, SmartRouter, RegExpRouter, TrieRouter, BlankEnv, BlankSchema, Env, Schema, HonoOptions.
+- Simple note: Constructor sets up router with SmartRouter as default, falling back to multiple router strategies.
+
+## src/hono-base.ts
+- Purpose: Base module containing the Hono class implementation with request handling, routing dispatch, middleware support, and request/response methods.
+- Names noticed: HonoBase, Context, Router, METHODS, Compose, Context, getPath, mergePath, HonoOptions, RouterRoute, HandlerInterface, MiddlewareHandlerInterface, NotFoundHandler, ErrorHa
+ndler.
+- Simple note: Contains main request lifecycle methods (fetch, request, fire), routing logic via #dispatch, and utility methods like basePath, route, mount.
+
+## src/router/pattern-router/index.ts
+- Purpose: Module export file that re-exports PatternRouter class for use by applications.
+- Names noticed: PatternRouter.
+- Simple note: Index file with module documentation, re-exports the main router class.
+
+## src/router/pattern-router/router.ts
+- Purpose: PatternRouter implementation using precompiled RegExp patterns for route matching.
+- Names noticed: PatternRouter, Route, Params, Result, Router, METHOD_NAME_ALL, UnsupportedPathError.
+- Simple note: Converts route paths to RegExps at add time, then does simple regex matching in match; handles wildcards and named parameters.
+
+## src/router/linear-router/index.ts
+- Purpose: Module export file that re-exports LinearRouter class for use by applications.
+- Names noticed: LinearRouter.
+- Simple note: Index file with module documentation, re-exports the main router class.
+
+## src/router/linear-router/router.ts
+- Purpose: LinearRouter implementation that stores routes as arrays and iteratively matches them.
+- Names noticed: LinearRouter, Params, Result, Router, METHOD_NAME_ALL, UnsupportedPathError, emptyParams, ROUTES_LOOP.
+- Simple note: Direct iteration through route array, handles wildcards, labels, and path segments; supports optional parameters via checkOptionalParameter.
+
+## src/router/reg-exp-router/index.ts
+- Purpose: Module export file that re-exports RegExpRouter and PreparedRegExpRouter for use by applications.
+- Names noticed: RegExpRouter, PreparedRegExpRouter, buildInitParams, serializeInitParams.
+- Simple note: Index file exporting main RegExpRouter class and prepared router utilities for serialization.
+
+## src/router/reg-exp-router/router.ts
+- Purpose: RegExpRouter implementation using Trie-based structure to generate compiled regular expressions at build time.
+- Names noticed: RegExpRouter, ParamIndexMap, Router, MESSAGE_MATCHER_IS_ALREADY_BUILT, METHOD_NAME_ALL, UnsupportedPathError, Trie, Matcher, match, emptyParam, buildWildcardRegExp, buildMatcherFromPreprocessedRoutes.
+- Simple note: Builds compiled regex matcher at build time, stores routes internally, matches via compiled regex; uses Trie for organizing routes and building efficient regex.
+
+## src/router/reg-exp-router/matcher.ts
+- Purpose: Defines Matcher type and match function for RegExpRouter.
+- Names noticed: HandlerData, StaticMap, Matcher, MatcherMap, Result, Router, emptyParam, match.
+- Simple note: Matcher is tuple of RegExp, HandlerData array, and StaticMap; match function uses compiled regex to find handlers.
+
+## src/router/reg-exp-router/prepared-router.ts
+- Purpose: PreparedRegExpRouter for cases where routes are fixed at construction and can be serialized.
+- Names noticed: PreparedRegExpRouter, MatcherMap, RelocateMap, HandlerData, StaticMap, METHOD_NAME_ALL, buildInitParams, serializeInitParams.
+- Simple note: Accepts pre-built matchers, adds routes dynamically to existing matcher, supports serialization for bundler usage.
+
+## src/router/reg-exp-router/node.ts
+- Purpose: Node class for Trie structure used in RegExpRouter to organize route patterns.
+- Names noticed: Node, ParamAssocArray, Context, PATH_ERROR, ONLY_WILDCARD_REG_EXP_STR, LABEL_REG_EXP_STR, compareKey.
+- Simple note: Trie node storing children by regexp pattern, supports varIndex for parameters; builds regexp string during traversal.
+
+## src/router/reg-exp-router/trie.ts
+- Purpose: Trie class that preprocesses paths into tokens and inserts them into Node structure.
+- Names noticed: Trie, ReplacementMap, Context, Node, RegEx, ParamAssocArray.
+- Simple note: Handles path tokenization including named groups, inserts into Node, builds compiled regex with parameter remapping.
+
+## src/router/smart-router/index.ts
+- Purpose: Module export file that re-exports SmartRouter class for use by applications.
+- Names noticed: SmartRouter.
+- Simple note: Index file with module documentation, re-exports the main router class.
+
+## src/router/smart-router/router.ts
+- Purpose: SmartRouter implementation that tries multiple routers and settles on the best one for a given request.
+- Names noticed: SmartRouter, Result, Router, MESSAGE_MATCHER_IS_ALREADY_BUILT, UnsupportedPathError, activeRouter.
+- Simple note: Wraps array of routers, tests each on registration then matching, delegates to first successful match, changes name to show active router.
+
+## src/router/trie-router/index.ts
+- Purpose: Module export file that re-exports TrieRouter class for use by applications.
+- Names noticed: TrieRouter.
+- Simple note: Index file with module documentation, re-exports the main router class.
+
+## src/router/trie-router/router.ts
+- Purpose: TrieRouter implementation using a tree structure for efficient route matching.
+- Names noticed: TrieRouter, Params, Result, Router, Node, emptyParams.
+- Simple note: Uses tree structure where each node has methods and children; traverse tree with path segments to find matching handlers.
+
+## src/router/trie-router/node.ts
+- Purpose: Node class for TrieRouter implementation storing methods, patterns, and children.
+- Names noticed: Node, HandlerSet, HandlerParamsSet, Pattern, Params, METHOD_NAME_ALL, splitRoutingPath, splitPath, getPattern, hasChildren.
+- Simple note: Tree nodes store multiple methods/handlers, patterns for wildcards and parameters; search traverses tree with path segments using BFS.
+
+## src/router/common.case.test.ts
+- Purpose: Shared test suite that all router implementations inherit to ensure consistent behavior.
+- Names noticed: runTest, RunnerTestSuite, Params, Router, ParamIndexMap.
+- Simple note: Common test cases covering basic usage, reserved words, wildcard, named params, multi-match, registration order, duplicate params, optional routes, global handlers.
+
+## src/router/pattern-router/router.test.ts
+- Purpose: Tests for PatternRouter including skipped edge cases and trailing slash support.
+- Names noticed: PatternRouter, UnsupportedPathError, runTest.
+- Simple note: Runs common test suite with skips for unsupported path patterns like duplicate params; tests trailing slash matching.
+
+## src/router/linear-router/router.test.ts
+- Purpose: Tests for LinearRouter including skipped cases due to its simpler matching logic.
+- Names noticed: LinearRouter, UnsupportedPathError, runTest.
+- Simple note: Runs common test suite with skips for multi-match with params per handler and capture regex with trailing wildcard; tests trailing slash.
+
+## src/router/reg-exp-router/router.test.ts
+- Purpose: Tests for RegExpRouter including unsupported path scenarios that trigger ambiguous regex errors.
+- Names noticed: RegExpRouter, UnsupportedPathError, runTest, ParamStash.
+- Simple note: Runs common test suite with skips for duplicate params and complex capturing groups; tests return value types and various unsupported path cases.
+
+## src/router/smart-router/router.test.ts
+- Purpose: Minimal test for SmartRouter using RegExpRouter and TrieRouter under the hood.
+- Names noticed: SmartRouter, RegExpRouter, TrieRouter, runTest.
+- Simple note: Runs common test suite through SmartRouter which adapts to use RegExpRouter and TrieRouter from its configuration.
+
+## src/router/trie-router/router.test.ts
+- Purpose: Tests for TrieRouter including standard common cases and node-specific tests.
+- Names noticed: TrieRouter, runTest.
+- Simple note: Runs common test suite directly on TrieRouter.
+
+## src/router/trie-router/node.test.ts
+- Purpose: Unit tests for Node class including root node, basic usage, named paths, wildcards, regex patterns, and multi-match scenarios.
+- Names noticed: Node.
+- Simple note: Extensive tests covering all aspects of the Node class including sorting order of matches, wildcard handling, regex patterns with named groups, multi-match scenarios, and duplicate parameter handling.
+
+## Cross-File Checklist
+
+- pattern-router: src/router/pattern-router/index.ts, src/router/pattern-router/router.ts, src/router/pattern-router/router.test.ts
+- linear-router: src/router/linear-router/index.ts, src/router/linear-router/router.ts, src/router/linear-router/router.test.ts
+- reg-exp-router: src/router/reg-exp-router/index.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/router.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/matcher.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/prepared-router.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/node.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/trie.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/router.test.ts
+- smart-router: src/router/smart-router/index.ts, src/router/smart-router/router.ts, src/router/smart-router/router.test.ts
+- trie-router: src/router/trie-router/index.ts, src/router/trie-router/router.ts, src/router/trie-router/node.ts, src/router/trie-router/node.test.ts, src/router/trie-router/router.test.ts

parts.text:
"Benchmark task: routing-read-write-ledger
Repository: https://github.com/honojs/hono.git

Create a new file at bench-artifacts/routing-read-write-ledger.md. This is a read/write endurance task, not a clever debugging task. First create the bench-artifacts directory if it does not exist. Then read every file in this exact list before writing the final document: src/hono.ts, src/hono-base.ts, src/router/pattern-router/index.ts, src/router/pattern-router/router.ts, src/router/linear-router/index.ts, src/router/linear-router/router.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/index.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/router.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/matcher.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/prepared-router.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/node.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/trie.ts, src/router/smart-router/index.ts, src/router/smart-router/router.ts, src/router/trie-router/index.ts, src/router/trie-router/router.ts, src/router/trie-router/node.ts, src/router/common.case.test.ts, src/router/pattern-router/router.test.ts, src/router/linear-router/router.test.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/router.test.ts, src/router/smart-router/router.test.ts, src/router/trie-router/router.test.ts, src/router/trie-router/node.test.ts. The output file must be long but mechanically simple. Start with a title '# Routing Read/Write Ledger'. For each listed file, add a section whose heading is exactly '## <file path>'. Under each heading write three bullets: '- Purpose: ...', '- Names noticed: ...', and '- Simple note: ...'. Keep the notes plain and safe; do not change source code. After the per-file sections, add '## Cross-File Checklist' with one bullet per router family: pattern-router, linear-router, reg-exp-router, smart-router, trie-router. Include exact file paths in the final answer and report that only bench-artifacts/routing-read-write-ledger.md was created.

Constraints:
- Do not ask the user to write prompts or inspect files manually.
- Prefer the smallest safe code change when edits are required.
- Include exact file paths, commands run, and verification status in the final answer."

This is a straightforward read/write task where I need to:
1. Create a bench-artifacts directory
2. Read all the listed files
3. Create a markdown file with sections for each file
4. Add a cross-file checklist

Let me start by creating the directory and reading all the files in parallel.
